steps.prompt: Validate the target audience and messaging for {product_name} launching on {launch_date}. Define: primary and secondary buyer personas, core pain points the product addresses, messaging hierarchy (primary value prop, supporting proof points, emotional benefit), channels where target customers spend time, and any objections or misconceptions to overcome at launch. Identify the ideal early adopter profile.

Define the MVP scope and launch requirements for {product_name} with a target launch of {launch_date}. Using audience-brief.md, produce: a prioritized feature list with must-have vs. nice-to-have classification, launch readiness criteria (product, legal, support, infrastructure), key risks and mitigations, success metrics and how they will be measured, and a high-level launch checklist by workstream (product, marketing, sales, support, comms).

checkpoint_config.message: Review launch-scope.md. Confirm the feature scope, success metrics, and readiness criteria before proceeding to campaign design.

steps.prompt: Design the launch campaign and channel strategy for {product_name} targeting {launch_date}. Use audience-brief.md and launch-scope.md. Produce: channel mix with rationale (paid, earned, owned), pre-launch build-up sequence (waitlist, teaser, announcement), launch-day activation plan, post-launch nurture strategy, budget allocation by channel, and KPIs per channel. Include contingency plans if early traction is below expectation.

Create the positioning and messaging framework for {product_name}. Use audience-brief.md and campaign-strategy.md. Deliver: a one-sentence positioning statement, tagline options (3-5 with rationale), brand voice guidance for launch communications (tone, vocabulary, what to avoid), key message pillars with supporting proof points, and a 'message house' document the whole team can use to stay on-brand across all launch channels.

Write the launch communications package for {product_name} launching on {launch_date}. Use messaging-framework.md and campaign-strategy.md. Produce: press release (inverted pyramid, with quotes), launch announcement email to existing audience, social media posts for launch day (LinkedIn, Twitter/X format — 3 variants each), a brief FAQ for customer support, and an internal announcement to the team. Maintain consistent voice per messaging-framework.md.

Build the complete launch timeline for {product_name} with target launch date {launch_date}. Use launch-scope.md, campaign-strategy.md, and launch-comms.md. Produce: a week-by-week timeline from now to 2 weeks post-launch, dependency map showing which workstreams block others, owner assignments by task (use role names, not individual names), a daily launch-week schedule with go/no-go decision points, and a post-launch review schedule (1-week, 1-month checkpoints).

checkpoint_config.message: Review the complete launch package: launch-scope.md, campaign-strategy.md, messaging-framework.md, launch-comms.md, and launch-timeline.md. Approve to execute the launch.
